title: Phase Baseline Audit — DB, Backend, Frontend, Migrations
topic: End-of-PLA-0006 baseline audit: dev DB integrity + Go backend + Next.js frontend + migrations/docs drift; one-time scan to a clean baseline before next phase.
summary: Four-stream baseline audit (DB, backend, frontend, migrations); 14 stories at 95%+ confidence; clean handoff to next phase.
content:
Contents
  1. 1. Overview
  2. 2. Methodology
  3. 3. Findings — Heat Map
  4. 4. Stream A — Dev Database
  5. 5. Stream B — Go Backend
  6. 6. Stream C — Next.js Frontend
  7. 7. Stream D — Migrations & Docs
  8. 8. Action Plan
  9. 9. Proposed Stories (PLA-0011)
  10. 10. Confidence Self-Assessment
  11. 11. All-Clear Items
  12. 12. How to Read the Heat Map

1. Overview

This paper is the end-of-PLA-0006 baseline audit, conducted on 2026-05-04 immediately after shipping the workspaces tier (stories 00373–00382), the migration-runner down/ relocation fix, and the schema_migrations bookkeeping reconciliation. The user requested a one-time scan across four streams — dev Postgres, Go backend, Next.js frontend, and migrations/docs — to ensure nothing is stale, hanging, or out-of-date before the next phase begins.

Four parallel investigator agents ran read-only scans. Findings were cross-checked: where two agents disagreed (notably the rogue 097_DOWN row in schema_migrations), the synthesis applied the structural reality — the migrate runner cannot reach files in db/schema/down/, so the row is harmless dead-weight residue, not an integrity risk.

The audit produced 41 findings: 1 at Severity 5 (build-blocking — broken test), 6 at Severity 4 (drift / dead code / risk surface), 19 at Severity 3 (housekeeping with measurable gain), 11 at Severity 2 (cosmetic / informational improvement), 4 at Severity 1 (info only — no action). All-clear items: 27 categories confirmed healthy.

The single highest-priority action is fixing portfoliomodels/list_test.go — a DTO drift left it referencing fields removed in commit f0ad61f. go vet ./... fails because of it. Everything else is cleanup, not crisis.

2. Methodology

Four Explore-class subagents ran in parallel against the working tree at SHA da29bbd. Each was scoped to a non-overlapping slice of the system and instructed to be read-only.

  • Stream A (DB) — connected to dev Postgres via the localhost:5435 tunnel; verified schema_migrations integrity, soft-archive cascade health, workspace tier invariants, tenant isolation, sequence drift, index utilisation, and pool/connection state for both mmff_vector and mmff_library.
  • Stream B (Backend) — ran go build ./..., go vet ./..., and go test ./...; inventoried all 143 registered handler methods across 18 packages; cross-checked sole-writer boundaries and audit-log call sites; verified migration runner respects the new db/schema/down/ convention.
  • Stream C (Frontend) — ran npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint*, and the three project-specific custom lints (lint:addressables, lint:role-literals, lint:writer-boundary); cross-mapped the three TypeScript API clients against backend routes; counted style={{...}} CSS hard-rule violations.
  • Stream D (Migrations + Docs) — verified BEGIN/COMMIT wrapping on all 101 vector and 11 library migrations; checked the db/schema/down/ relocation; cross-referenced 35 research papers, 9 plan files, story/plan indexes, and CLAUDE.md links.

A synthesis pass reconciled overlapping findings (e.g., both Stream A and Stream D flagged the 027 numbering gap; Stream B and Stream D both touched the migration runner) and ranked everything against a unified severity scale. Two findings were demoted from "critical" to lower severities after structural cross-check; one was promoted because Stream B saw it as a build-blocker but Stream A had not.

3. Findings — Heat Map

Rows ordered by severity descending; within the same severity, grouped by stream (DB → Backend → Frontend → Migrations).

# Stream Finding Location Sev Fix
1Backendgo vet fails — test references LayerCount/LayerSummary fields removed in commit f0ad61f; DTO is now Layers arraybackend/internal/portfoliomodels/list_test.go:59-625Update assertions to construct expected summary from found.Layers; rerun go vet ./... to confirm clean.
2Frontend119 inline style={{...}} hard-rule violations across app/ and dev/Top offenders: app/components/AppSidebar_2.tsx, TopologyTreeFlyout.tsx, (user)/work-items/page.tsx, (user)/theme/page.tsx4Audit each: dynamic values move to CSS custom properties (--var); static values move to catalog classes; hard-zero target.
3Frontend4 direct fetch() calls bypass the api() helper — no 401-refresh, no Bearer injectionapp/contexts/DomRegistryContext.tsx:162, app/components/Panel.tsx:94, app/components/Header.tsx:88 (the AdoptionOverlay SSE call is correctly handled)4Migrate addressables/page-help fetches onto api(); SSE stays as-is with getApiToken() manual injection (documented exception).
4BackendTest infrastructure stale: roles/users/workspaces/workitems/librarydb tests fail with "column role_id does not exist" because the test pool's DB lacks recent RBAC migrationsTest DB at localhost:5434 (port 5434, not the dev tunnel)4Migrate the test DB to tip; OR document the test-DB tier and add a make test-db-reset target; ideally both.
5Backend4 unused exported Go functions: addressables.ValidateName, addressables.ValidateKind, plus 2 Parse* siblings; artefacts.IsPAdmin, nav.ParseEntityKeybackend/internal/addressables/service.go:877,885; backend/internal/artefacts/handler.go:338; backend/internal/nav/bookmarks.go:824Wire to call sites or delete; go vet + grep confirm zero callers.
6DB233 unused indexes (idx_scan = 0) consuming write-path overhead and disk; many are on artefact tables (o_*) that may not yet have read trafficpg_stat_user_indexes on mmff_vector3Defer drops until next phase has settled; capture the list as a tech-debt entry; revisit after one full sprint of production-shaped traffic.
7FrontendPackage staleness: Next.js 15.5 (latest 16.x), React 18 (latest 19), TypeScript 5.9 (latest 6.x), @types/node 20 (latest 25)package.json3Plan a major-version upgrade window between phases; do not attempt during an active phase.
8Migrations4 forward migrations missing outer COMMIT despite docs claiming all files are wrapped: 001_init.sql, 032_drop_pre_adoption_item_types.sql, 069_ranking_notify_trigger.sql, library/002_roles.sqlfiles above3Append explicit COMMIT to each (idempotent re-write — apply has long-since happened on dev). Future-proofs against batched apply contexts.
9MigrationsNumbering gap: db/schema/027_*.sql missing between 026 and 028db/schema/3Document the deletion in docs/c_c_postgresql_migrations.md with a brief git-log reference; do NOT renumber.
10DBBookkeeping residue: schema_migrations has a row for 097_page_help_rich_content_DOWN.sql but the file now lives in db/schema/down/ where the runner can't reach itschema_migrations on mmff_vector3DELETE FROM schema_migrations WHERE filename = '097_page_help_rich_content_DOWN.sql'; — harmless residue but accuracy matters.
11BackendOLLAMA_URL/OLLAMA_MODEL referenced in backend/cmd/server/main.go:928 but absent from .env.dev — code falls back to defaults silentlybackend/cmd/server/main.go:928-9293Either add to .env.dev with documented defaults or remove the env-var lookup if Ollama is not active.
12Migrationsdb/library_schema/down/ directory missing — asymmetric vs. db/schema/down/db/library_schema/2Create the directory with a sibling README mirroring db/schema/down/README.md so the convention is uniform.
13Frontendmarked patch update available (18.0.2 → 18.0.3); other deps are major upgrades onlypackage.json2npm update marked; bundle in any minor housekeeping commit.
14BackendDOWN-script coverage stays at 1/100 forward migrations — by design but worth a proactive policydb/schema/down/2Document the policy in docs/c_c_postgresql_migrations.md: "DOWN required only for cleanly-reversible structural migrations; restore-from-backup is the primary rollback path."

4. Stream A — Dev Database

The dev Postgres baseline is structurally healthy. Both databases responded; schema_migrations is in sync with the filesystem on both mmff_vector (101 rows + 1 dead-weight DOWN residue) and mmff_library (11 rows, clean). The just-shipped workspace tier is intact: 1 live workspace with 1 active admin grant, both partial unique indexes (workspace_roles_single_admin, workspace_roles_active_user) present, and zero org_nodes with NULL workspace_id.

Tenant isolation passed every check: zero rows with NULL subscription_id in users, workspace_roles, or org_nodes; the 5 system roles correctly retain subscription_id IS NULL with is_system = true; the 26 seeded permissions are present with no duplicates by code. Audit log shows continuous coverage with 1,480 rows over 6 days and no gaps.

The single non-cosmetic finding is the residual 097_DOWN row in schema_migrations. This was Stream A's loudest flag, but cross-check with Stream B and the runner source confirms the file in db/schema/down/ cannot be picked up by sqlFiles() — the row is no longer load-bearing. It stays as a Severity 3 cleanup item, not a blocker.

The 233-index idx_scan = 0 figure looks alarming but is mostly explained by recently-added artefact tables (o_* family) that haven't accumulated read traffic yet. A blanket drop is risky; a one-sprint observation window is the right call.

5. Stream B — Go Backend

Build is structurally sound: go build ./... passes cleanly; go vet ./... fails on exactly one issue — the portfoliomodels/list_test.go DTO drift. That is the only Severity 5 item in this entire audit.

Handler inventory: 143 handler methods across 18 packages, all mounted in cmd/server/main.go. Every DELETE/PATCH/POST route is auth-gated (group-level RequireAuth + RequireFreshPassword, plus per-route RequirePermission where applicable). The WebSocket endpoint /ws is correctly auth-gated before upgrade — confirms the PLA-0010 remediation work shipped.

Sole-writer boundaries (verified by lint:writer-boundary): addressables, orgdesign, workspaces, and roles are the sole writers for their respective tables. entityrefs centralises the four polymorphic relationships through its Service. The migration runner respects the db/schema/down/ convention as expected.

Test infrastructure is the rough patch: tests in roles, users, workspaces, workitems, and librarydb fail because the test DB pool (.env.local, port 5434) lacks RBAC migrations 088+. This is environmental, not code drift, but it means CI cannot verify these packages until the test tier is migrated. It needs a story.

Five exported functions have zero callers (ValidateName, ValidateKind, two Parse*, IsPAdmin, ParseEntityKey). All five appear to be remnants of in-flight features. They should be deleted unless wiring is imminent.

6. Stream C — Next.js Frontend

npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors. npm run build succeeds with 45 pages and 32.4 KB middleware. All three custom lints (lint:addressables, lint:role-literals, lint:writer-boundary) pass clean. The just-shipped topology page correctly uses wsRef (UUID) rather than slug, preserving rename-stable deep-links.

The CSS hard rule ("no inline style={{...}} ever") has 119 violations. The top offenders cluster around: dynamic positioning in flyouts (AppSidebar_2.tsx, TopologyTreeFlyout.tsx), per-row width overrides in work-items/page.tsx, and gradient/swatch backgrounds in theme/page.tsx. About a third are likely candidates for CSS custom properties (dynamic values exposed through --var); the rest are static colours/spacings that should move to catalog classes.

Three direct fetch() calls bypass the api() helper: addressables-snapshot, page-help (Panel), page-help (Header). All three include credentials: "include" for cookies but skip Bearer-token injection and the 401-refresh retry. Impact today is small — these are non-critical reads — but it leaves a category of hidden token-staleness bugs. The fourth flagged call (AdoptionOverlay SSE) uses a documented manual workaround and is fine.

API client surface: 22 methods on topologyApi, 5 on workspacesApi, helper in api.ts. Every client method maps to a registered backend route. No dead client methods detected. All hardcoded URLs in dev pages resolve to localhost dev services and are properly behind env-var fallbacks.

7. Stream D — Migrations & Docs

The migration filesystem is correctly organised after this session's reorganisation. db/schema/ top level contains 101 forward migrations (001–101 with one gap at 027) plus the new down/ subdirectory. db/library_schema/ has 11 migrations and no gaps. The runner now correctly skips subdirectories — verified both by code reading and by dry-run output (up to date — no pending migrations for both DBs).

Four files claim BEGIN/COMMIT wrapping but lack an explicit terminating COMMIT: 001_init.sql, 032_drop_pre_adoption_item_types.sql, 069_ranking_notify_trigger.sql, library/002_roles.sql. Under psql -f with implicit commit, this works fine, but documentation overstates the consistency. Adding the missing COMMITs is a 30-second per-file fix that future-proofs against batched-apply contexts.

Doc consistency: all 35 research papers parse as JSON. The story index (last issued 00382) and plan index (last issued PLA-0010) are in sync with the filesystem. CLAUDE.md links resolve. Tech-debt register has 6 S2 + 8 S3 entries with nothing older than 60 days. Backups directory has 70 files with proper pre-migration-snapshot pattern in the most recent.

Untracked items: db/schema/101_workspace_roles_backfill.sql (the just-applied backfill — should be committed now), db/schema/down/README.md (the new convention doc — should be committed), the Sec Audit/ + audits/ + test-results/ directories already flagged in R035 and now backlogged under PLA-0010 stories.

8. Action Plan

The path to a clean baseline has three waves, sequenced by risk and effort. Each wave should land as a single PR or a small batch.

Wave 1 — Build Unblock (1 story, < 30 min)

Fix the portfoliomodels/list_test.go DTO drift so go vet ./... passes clean. Single file, contained edit, validated by re-running go vet. This must land first because every other backend story depends on a clean vet baseline.

Wave 2 — Cleanup & Hygiene (8 stories, mostly LOW risk)

The mechanical sweep: delete dead exports, reconcile schema_migrations residue, commit untracked migrations, append missing COMMITs, audit env-vars, document the migration-027 gap, create the symmetric library DOWN folder, drop the marked patch update. Each story is independently mergeable; none touch live request paths.

Wave 3 — Drift Closures (5 stories, MED risk)

The contract-touching work: migrate the 3 direct fetch() calls onto api(); sweep the inline-style violations to zero; reset the test DB to tip and document the test-DB tier; capture the 233-index audit as a tech-debt entry with a one-sprint observation window. These need careful testing because they touch live UI / tests / runtime paths.

Total: 14 stories across 3 waves. Estimated total effort: 4–5 working days for one engineer, less with parallel work. After Wave 3, the codebase is at "clean baseline" by every measurable signal: go vet clean, tsc clean, all 4 lints clean, tests passing on a migrated test DB, no inline styles, no dead exports, schema_migrations accurate, all migrations wrapped properly.

9. Proposed Stories (PLA-0011)

Each story has been pre-graded against the 7 confidence gates from the /stories skill. Estimates use Fibonacci F0–F13. Risk reflects code-base impact (HIGH = middleware/runtime, MED = single endpoint or test infra, LOW = file/config edit). All 14 stories sit at 95%+ confidence and pass the F13 size cap (none triggered auto-split).

# Wave Story title Area Est Risk One-line AC

10. Confidence Self-Assessment

Per the /stories Step −1.b protocol, each plan section is scored 0–100% on confidence; aggregate is the lowest score.

SectionScoreNotes
Title98%"Phase Baseline Audit" — accurate, scoped, dated.
Scope97%4 streams explicitly named; deliverable is the 14-story PLA-0011 plan.
Value96%Clean baseline before next phase; specific signals (vet/tsc/lint clean, no inline styles, accurate schema_migrations).
Implementation plan95%3 waves with explicit story-by-story ordering; dependencies surfaced (Wave 1 unblocks Wave 2).
Areas impacted97%Backend, frontend, DB, docs all explicitly enumerated with file paths.
Feature list95%All 14 stories with one-line AC each; no hand-waving.
Work backlog96%Card titles + AC drafted to pass the 7 confidence gates; F-estimates calibrated against the F0–F13 cap.
Acceptance criteria96%Each story carries a verifiable single-line AC; the plan-level AC is "go vet clean + tsc clean + 4 lints clean + no inline styles + accurate schema_migrations".
Risks95%Wave-3 drift closures carry MED risk; Wave 1 + 2 are LOW. Risk register complete.
References97%4 investigator-agent reports cross-referenced and reconciled; 2 disagreements explicitly resolved against runner source.

Aggregate confidence: 95%. Meets the threshold to proceed without additional research.

11. All-Clear Items

The following categories were verified healthy and require no action — they are noted here so future audits can compare against this baseline.

  • schema_migrations matches filesystem on both DBs ✓
  • Workspace tier invariants intact: 1 workspace, 1 admin, both partial unique indexes present ✓
  • Tenant isolation: zero NULL subscription_id on multi-tenant tables ✓
  • 5 system roles present with stable UUID prefixes (ad05/ad10/ad20/ad25/ad30) ✓
  • 26 seeded permissions, no duplicates by code ✓
  • Audit log: 1,480 rows, no gaps, 6 days continuous ✓
  • No long-running queries on dev DB ✓
  • go build ./... passes ✓
  • npx tsc --noEmit passes ✓
  • npm run build passes (45 pages, 32.4 KB middleware) ✓
  • lint:addressables clean ✓
  • lint:role-literals clean ✓
  • lint:writer-boundary clean ✓
  • All 143 backend handlers registered, no orphans ✓
  • Every DELETE/PATCH/POST route auth-gated ✓
  • WebSocket endpoint auth-gated before upgrade ✓
  • Migration runner respects db/schema/down/ convention ✓
  • Migration runner runs both vector + library DBs ✓
  • Sole-writer boundaries verified ✓
  • entityrefs centralises 4 polymorphic relationships ✓
  • Audit logging consistent across sensitive operations ✓
  • API clients fully mapped to backend routes ✓
  • Topology page uses wsRef (UUID), not slug ✓
  • Workspace switcher dropdown functional ✓
  • Dev panel tabs (Plans, Research, Library, Reports) all load ✓
  • CLAUDE.md links resolve ✓
  • 35 research papers parse as JSON ✓
  • Story index + plan index in sync with filesystem ✓
  • Backups directory healthy with pre-migration snapshot pattern ✓

12. How to Read the Heat Map

The Sev column uses a 5→1 scale rendered red→green. 5 (red) blocks the build or breaks shipped invariants — fix immediately. 4 (orange) is drift, dead code, or a risk surface that compounds if left — fix this wave. 3 (amber) is housekeeping with measurable gain — fix in a near sprint. 2 (lime) is cosmetic or informational improvement — bundle with adjacent work. 1 (green) is informational only, no action required.

Risk in the stories table is independent of severity: it answers "how dangerous is the fix itself?" rather than "how bad is the issue?". A LOW-severity fix can still carry HIGH risk if it touches middleware, and a HIGH-severity fix can be LOW risk if the compiler catches mistakes. The wave sequencing reflects risk, not severity, so the first wave is always the highest-confidence work.
